questions.question_text: Becca is developing a data model that will be used to implement a relational database. What data model would Becca choose?

answers.text: A conceptual class diagram
answers.explanation: Conceptual diagrams are independent of any solution or technology and are primarily used to establish a consistent vocabulary describing business information. Class diagrams are used to support object-oriented software, which is not mentioned in the question

answers.text: A logical or physical entity-relationship diagram
answers.explanation: Entity-Relationship Diagrams are the most common model for helping build relational databases

answers.text: A logical or physical class diagram
answers.explanation: Class diagrams are used to support object-oriented software development vs. relational databases

answers.text: A conceptual entity-relationship diagram
answers.explanation: Conceptual diagrams are independent of any solution or technology and are primarily used to establish a consistent vocabulary describing business information

questions.question_text: Given important stakeholders in this organization are family members, what do you need to do differently to define their roles and responsibilities?

answers.text: Nothing. You should find an organizational chart to consider any unique needs.
answers.explanation: It may require a bit more tact when defining roles and responsibilities in a family-run organization. But using a technique such as organizational modeling works for a family business as well as for any other organization. However, there is nothing in the case study to indicate an organizational chart exists, making it a good distractor (and partially correct). The BABOK spells out that it is used “to determine if the organizational units or people listed have any unique needs and interests that should be considered.”

answers.text: Understand that despite the titles family members have, the parents will have ultimate decision-making authority.
answers.explanation: This is a false assumption. It may be true in some family-run organizations, but it is still an assumption. It would be better to conduct stakeholder analysis to clarify assumptions.

answers.text: Nothing. You should identify the family members (and their characteristics) who will be impacted by the change as you normally would.
answers.explanation: It may require a bit more tact when defining roles and responsibilities in a family-run organization. This answer is essentially the definition of stakeholder analysis, an important element in planning stakeholder engagement and partially correct. The BABOK says “Stakeholder analysis involves identifying the stakeholders (who will be directly or indirectly impacted by the change)”

answers.text: Use a Roles and Permission Matrix for this effort.
answers.explanation: This type of technique does help define roles. But it only refers to who can Create-Read-Update-Delete (CRUD) data and other resources. It surprisingly is only referenced in task 8.4, Assess Enterprise Limitations and is too solution-focused to apply here

questions.question_text: You recognize the business need statements could be improved with additional refinements. If you wanted to improve them, what would you suggest be changed or added to the stated business needs?

answers.text: The sponsors are worried that the unique Willow Brook experience will deteriorate for the franchisees and want to implement a Net Promoter Score survey to measure public and industry perception.
answers.explanation: A measurement tool like Net Promoter Score could help measure and quantity perception, but it is not a business need. It is also not a technique referenced in the BABOK. A business need is “A problem or opportunity of strategic or tactical importance to be addressed.” BABOK Glossary

answers.text: Adding a new franchisee is a complicated and error-prone process that takes up to a week to perform and the franchisees would prefer it be done in two days.
answers.explanation: This may be a franchisee desire, but there is nothing in the case study to support it. How did the senior BA arrive at two days? More analysis would perhaps clarify the actual preference.

answers.text: Franchisee growth rates have slowed from 635% 4-5 years ago to 35% the past two years.
answers.explanation: Calculating the growth 4-5 years ago: 635% - ((147-20)/20). 1-2 years ago: 35% = ((354-262)/262). Business need is an input to BABOK tasks 3.1 and 3.2 among others. See the Glossary.

answers.text: Franchisee growth rates have slowed from 371% 4-5 years ago to 34% the past two years.
answers.explanation: This answer erroneously adds the growth rates for 4 and 5 years ago: 96% + 275% = 371% and 1 and 2 years ago: 6% + 28% = 34%.

questions.question_text: You feel strongly about continuously improving your business analysis efforts. When you identify potential improvements, which of the following roles is the most important one to consult with and why?

answers.text: Shelly and Stan, the sponsors, since they will have to approve any additional funding needed.
answers.explanation: True, the sponsors play a role in business analysis performance improvements since they may ultimately have to make funding or time decisions. But the PM is the better answer

answers.text: Chis, a key Subject Matter Expert, to set expectations regarding his involvement.
answers.explanation: True, Domain Subject Matter experts play a role in business analysis performance improvements. Chris appears to be a Domain SME, one of the listed stakeholders for business analysis performance improvements. But like the answer for the sponsors, the PM is the better choice

answers.text: Project Manager, since any potential corrections will impact the project’s schedule and budget.
answers.explanation: All the possible answers in this question are potential stakeholders involved with business analysis performance improvements. But because of the effect on the project, the PM is the best choice. “If potential problems or opportunities for improvement are identified, the project manager must be consulted before changes are implemented to assess whether those changes will have an impact on the project.”

answers.text: Garrett, the assumed CIO and a key Subject Matter Expert, since any improvements will affect IT.
answers.explanation: True, Domain Subject Matter experts play a role in business analysis performance improvements. It could be argued that Garrett is more of an Implementation Subject Matter Expert. Plus, not all improvements would involve IT

questions.question_text: Given the entrepreneurial nature of the company, which of the following should you consider for measuring the business analysis work effort?

answers.text: Ask key stakeholders which performance measures they think are appropriate.
answers.explanation: The case study describes an environment that sounds informal so asking key stakeholders for their input would be appropriate. “The business analyst may also elicit assessment measures from stakeholders.”

answers.text: Use Item Tracking because of its simplicity.
answers.explanation: True, Item Tracking is a listed technique for the task Identify Business Analysis Performance Improvements. But there are several other possible techniques to consider, making this answer overly narrow

answers.text: Use standard business analysis performance measures that have worked on other similar projects.
answers.explanation: True, using standard measures that have worked for you before makers sense. But, this may not be suitable for an entrepreneurial, family-owned type of business. Minimally you would discuss your standard measures, but asking stakeholders is an even better answer.

answers.text: Use existing organizational performance standards.
answers.explanation: Organizational Performance Standards is a guideline for the task Identify Business Analysis Performance Improvements but would not be the best answer for this situation. The case study describes an entrepreneurial company that probably lacks this type of formality

questions.question_text: The following are some of the factors a business analyst would consider to establish the level of decomposition and the level of detail to be specified while conducting information decomposition. Which is NOT?

answers.text: Organizational standards.
answers.explanation: This is one of the factors

answers.text: Contractual or regulatory obligations.
answers.explanation: This is one of the factors

answers.text: Knowledge and understanding of the stakeholders.
answers.explanation: This is one of the factors

answers.text: Schedule and budget allocations.
answers.explanation: Though as a business analyst you want to meet your deadlines, time should not be the main factor in determining the level of decomposition

answers.text: Focus group; quantitative
answers.explanation: A focus group is a means to elicit ideas and opinions about a specific product, service, or opportunity in an interactive group environment; participants are not anonymous. Quantitative measures are numerical, countable, or finite; focus groups result in qualitative measures. Close-ended questions do result in quantitative measures part of the answer is correct

answers.text: Survey; quantitative
answers.explanation: A survey or questionnaire is used to elicit business analysis information from a group of people in a structured way and in a relatively short period of time. Quantitative measures are numerical, countable, or finite, and can be elicited through close-ended questions in surveys

answers.text: Group interview; qualitative
answers.explanation: Group interview is not a term referred to in the BABOK. Qualitative measures are subjective and are elicited through open-ended questions in surveys or interviews; the question refers to close-ended questions which result in quantitative measures

answers.text: Group interview; quantitative
answers.explanation: Group interview is not a term referred to in the BABOK. Quantitative measures are numerical, countable, or finite, usually involving amounts, quantities, or rates. Close-ended questions do result in quantitative measures so part of the answer is correct

questions.question_text: Megan is the business analyst for an organization and she's coaching Lindsey, a new business analyst, on the importance of identifying and recording key dependencies between requirements. Which one of the following is the primary reason why key dependencies should be identified and recorded?

answers.text: To identify the benefit of each requirement.
answers.explanation: Benefit is a factor that influences the traceability effort, but not is a reason why dependencies need to be identified and recorded

answers.text: To identify the relative risk of each requirement.
answers.explanation: Risk is a factor that influences the traceability effort, but not is a reason why dependencies need to be identified and recorded

answers.text: To determine who owns the requirement in the project life cycle.
answers.explanation: Determining who owns the requirement is not a reason by key dependencies need to be identified and recorded

answers.text: To help achieve efficiency by implementing related requirements at the same time.
answers.explanation: Identifying and documenting dependencies assists the business analyst in sequencing project work activities to design and deploy the capabilities found in the requirements. It also assists the business analyst in correctly allocating requirements to solution components

questions.question_text: Before recommending a change to the inventory system, what should the team have done?

answers.text: Ensured that requirements were clearly communicated along with cost and benefits information to the key stakeholders responsible for approving the proposal.
answers.explanation: This answer describes an important part of approving requirements (task 5.5). However, the enterprise and solution limitations need to be understood before recommending solution improvements and getting their approval.

answers.text: Understood enterprise and solution limitations.
answers.explanation: These two items are inputs to task 8.5, Recommend Actions to Increase Solution Value, which is what the scenario is describing

answers.text: Provided a clear definition of the outcomes that will satisfy the business need.
answers.explanation: This answer is partially correct since it is part of defining the future state (task 6.2), but this answer could apply to any change. The scenario describes evaluating an existing solution and proposing improvements, part of task 8.5, and relies on specific inputs.

answers.text: Ensured that all requirements and designs align to the business requirements and support the delivery of needed value.
answers.explanation: This answer is partially correct since it describes validating requirements (task 7.3), but this answer could apply to any change. The scenario describes evaluating an existing solution and proposing improvements, part of task 8.5, and relies on specific inputs.

questions.question_text: What is the expected Return on Investment for the proposed inventory management change?

answers.explanation: This answer omits the $75,000 installation cost in the benefits minus cost. ((80000+110000+180000)-200,000) / 275,000 = .62, which is incorrect.
This answer represents the net of the benefits minus the expected costs. ((80000+110000+180000)-275000) = 95,000. It is only part of the calculation for the Return on Investment.

The formula in the BABOK for Return on Investment is “((Total Benefits – Cost of the Investment) / Cost of the Investment).” The numbers in the scenario are: ((80000+110000+180000)-275000) / 275000, which equals .35. Please note the BABOK has some inconsistency in how they define ROI. Their formula above differs from their word explanation, which is: “the net benefits divided by the cost of the change.” “Cost of the change” could be construed as just costs to create and implement a solution, but “Cost of the Investment” would include ongoing costs to support a solution (Total Cost of Ownership), so we suggest using the formula to calculate ROI
This is close, but divides by the $200,000 development costs instead of the total cost of $275,000. ((80000+110000+180000) - 275000) / 200000 = .48

answers.text: Obtain approval of requirements and designs for solution construction to proceed.
answers.explanation: This answer essentially describes the purpose of the task Approve Requirements. While that will eventually have to be done, a more urgent need is to facilitate collaboration amongst the stakeholders involved

answers.text: Ensure that all requirements and designs in the recommended solution align to the business requirements and support the delivery of needed value.
answers.explanation: This answer essentially describes the purpose of the task Validate Requirements. While that will eventually have to be done, a more urgent need is to facilitate collaboration amongst the stakeholders involved

answers.text: Encourage stakeholders to work towards the common goal of increased profitability.

answers.text: Ask the sponsor to decide which kind of optimization to do first.
answers.explanation: This may be a practical solution, but it is not prescribed in the BABOK. Once the recommendation is made, its output is an input to Manage Stakeholder Collaboration, making another answer more correct.

questions.question_text: You are the business analyst for an initiative that has recently experienced changes in key business stakeholders, including governance personnel. answers.text: Domain knowledge

answers.text: Solution scope

questions.question_text: You are a business analyst assigned to your first agile project. To better prepare, you are researching the role of the business analyst in the world of agile. You come across a blog authored by a self-proclaimed expert in the field of agile who states the role of the business analyst is limited and will soon be eliminated in agile projects. Which of the following best refutes that statement?

answers.text: The business analyst role will continue to be performed within agile projects, though the scope of the work and tasks within the current framework will likely change.
answers.explanation: It is true that the BA role will continue to be performed within agile projects but tasks will not change; rather, they are approached and performed somewhat differently in agile projects

answers.text: Business analysis is a stated role; anyone who performs business analysis tasks can be considered a business analyst.
answers.explanation: Business analysis is not a role. Business analyst is a role, but not business analysis, so this answer cannot be correct. Also, while the rest of the statement is true, it doesn't best address the scenario and related question

answers.text: Business analysis is performed through many varying perspectives, one of which is agile. All of the tasks described in the BABOK are relevant within the context of agile, but they may be performed somewhat differently.
answers.explanation: discusses agile as one of the perspectives in the BABOK; moreover, agile approaches are taken into consideration when planning the business analysis approach

answers.text: The blogger is correct that the role of the business analyst is not prescribed and eventually will not be needed on agile projects.
answers.explanation: While there may not be an agile team member with the job title of "business analyst", the tasks of business analysis are critical and will remain so on all projects

questions.question_text: Which of the following best describes the difference between synchronous and asynchronous call message types in a sequence diagram?

answers.text: A synchronous call can "synch" to many objects at a time allowing for multiple conversations; an asynchronous call is unable to "synch" meaning it is temporarily offline.
answers.explanation: A totally invented answer

answers.text: A synchronous call transfers control to the receiving object; the sender cannot act until a return message is received. An asynchronous call allows the sending object to continue with its own processing while waiting for an answer.

An asynchronous call transfers control to the receiving object; the sender cannot act until a return message is received. A synchronous call allows the sending object to continue with its own processing while waiting for an answer.
answers.explanation: Just the opposite

answers.text: These terms refer to "signals" rather than message types. However, the signals in a synchronous call allows an object to receive messages in order from the sender.
answers.explanation: "Signal" is another name for an asynchronous call. The rest of the answer is made up

questions.question_text: Robert is struggling to find the best way to model requirements on his project. answers.text: Process modeling
answers.explanation: Process models might help with the larger business process, not the particular goal of saving and resuming a mortgage

answers.text: Use Case Diagram

answers.text: Review reports to understand exactly how much customer satisfaction scores have decreased.

answers.text: Analyze key stakeholders to understand their power/interest level and current level of engagement.
answers.explanation: This might be appropriate after consensus is reached on the problem definition

answers.text: Collaborate with key stakeholders to gain consensus on the definition of the problem and resulting impacts.
answers.explanation: Collaboration is key and getting buy-in to the definition of the problem and identifying the business need is always a first step

answers.text: Internal rate of return, cost-benefit analysis

answers.text: Payback period, value realization
answers.explanation: These are financial analysis technique elements

questions.question_text: Which type of models can be used to define and document the business analysis approach?

answers.text: Data
answers.explanation: Data models are not utilized in the plan business analysis approach task. , 7.1.6, 7.4.6, and 10.15

answers.text: Scope
answers.explanation: Scope models determine the boundaries of the solution as an input to planning and estimating

answers.text: Process
answers.explanation: Process models can be used to define and document the business analysis approach

answers.text: Object
answers.explanation: There is no such model stated in the BABOK

questions.question_text: For effective review, objectives are clearly communicated to all participants prior to the review. Which of the following is NOT an objective for reviews?

answers.text: To educate reviewers about the work product.
answers.explanation: True.

answers.text: To answer a question, resolve an issue, or explore alternatives.
answers.explanation: True.

answers.text: To produce numerous new ideas from participants.
answers.explanation: Reviews may generate new ideas but , the main objective is to evaluate an existing work product

answers.text: To measure work product quality.
answers.explanation: True.

questions.question_text: Which of the following best represents an example of a qualitative benefit?

answers.text: Total cost of ownership.
answers.explanation: The total cost of ownership (TCO) is the cost to acquire a solution, the cost of using the solution, and the cost of supporting the solution for the foreseeable future and is a quantitative measurement

answers.text: Improved customer satisfaction.
answers.explanation: This is a qualitative measure that can add significant value to the organization. But, is difficult to quantify

answers.text: Shorter payback period.
answers.explanation: This is a return-on-investment or quantitative measurement

answers.text: Improved return on investment.
answers.explanation: Return on investment (ROI) measures the gain from an investment relative to its cost captured as percentage. ROI is a quantitative measure. BABOK

questions.question_text: How are any financial analysis and value assessments validated?

answers.text: By obtaining sponsor approval of the stated value assumptions.
answers.explanation: Close. Sponsor approval is evidence of acceptance but not value validation.

answers.text: By relating them back to strategic goals and objectives.
answers.explanation: Financial analysis and value assessments must align to the strategic goals and objectives of the enterprise

answers.text: By relating them back to business needs.
answers.explanation: Business needs are an input to Analyze Current State and typically serve as a launching point for BA work

answers.text: By relating them back to business requirements.
answers.explanation: Business requirements are an output of Analyze Current State and consist of business problem, opportunity, and constraints

questions.question_text: Which of the following accurately describes the difference between business knowledge and industry knowledge?

answers.text: Business Knowledge requires an understanding of fundamental business principles while Industry Knowledge requires an understanding of the market context surrounding the business. analysis work
answers.explanation: Great sounding answer, but it is invented

answers.text: Business Knowledge and Industry Knowledge are fundamentally the same. Both require fundamental understanding of the principles surrounding the context in which business analysis work is done.
answers.explanation: Great sounding answer, but it is invented. Industry Knowledge is one component of Business Knowledge

answers.text: Business knowledge is one component of industry knowledge. There are several further components of business knowledge including understanding and providing value and the context (business climate) in which organizations function.
answers.explanation: Just the opposite is true. Industry knowledge is one component of Business knowledge and the rest of the answer is invented

answers.text: Industry Knowledge is one component of Business Knowledge.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT an element in item tracking technique?

answers.text: Item Record.
answers.explanation: This is a listed element

answers.text: Item Identifier.
answers.explanation: Not an element but an attribute for item tracking

answers.text: Item Management.
answers.explanation: This is a listed element

answers.text: Metrics.
answers.explanation: This is a listed element

questions.question_text: As a business analyst you want to specify the needs of a specific user and enable teams to define features of value to a user and written in a user's language. What technique would best serve this purpose?

answers.text: Use cases and scenarios
answers.explanation: These are used to model the desired behavior of a solution by showing user interactions with the solution to achieve a specific goal.  and

answers.text: User stories
answers.explanation: User stories are a technique used in the specify and model requirements task to specify requirements as a statement about what people do or need to do when using the solution

answers.text: Prototyping
answers.explanation: This technique is used to assist the user in visualizing the appearance and capabilities of a planned solution

answers.text: User specification
answers.explanation: There is no such technique in the BABOK. The correct answer is user stories

questions.question_text: Stacy is a Business System analyst assigned to a new management reporting initiative. Stacy has noted that a few of the current managers are bit reluctant to make any changes to the existing management reports and may not be cooperative while sharing their requirements about the management reports. What should Stacy do manage those reluctant stakeholders?

answers.text: As stakeholders are from Senior Management team, Stacy should discuss the matter with the Sponsor and seek his/her help to sort this out.
answers.explanation: As a Business Analyst it is Stacy’s responsibility to ensure that all relevant stakeholders will remain positively engaged with the project for entire project life cycle. Stacy may seek out the sponsor on other stakeholder related issues.

answers.text: Stay should explore lesson learned documents from previous projects to find a solution from experiences of similar situation in any past project.
answers.explanation: This is partly correct and Stacy may follow this approach to learn a lesson about stakeholder management, but ultimately she has to take necessary actions to sort out the issue.

answers.text: Stacy should discuss the matter with the Project Manager and seek his/her help to sort this out.
answers.explanation: As a Business Analyst it is Stacy’s responsibility to ensure that all relevant stakeholders will remain positively engaged with the project for entire project life cycle. Stacy may seek out the PM on other stakeholder related issues.

answers.text: Stacy should take actions to prepare the stakeholders for the change initiative and ensure their buy-in.
answers.explanation: In preparing for elicitation, the business analyst should ensure that there is buy-in from all necessary stakeholders

questions.question_text: Assessing stakeholder attitudes about the project is important. Which of the following best describes the factors to consider?

answers.text: Attitude towards collaboration, teamwork, and other stakeholders, and their job
answers.explanation: Attitude toward their job is not a listed factor

answers.text: Attitude toward the business goals, objective of the initiative, business analysis in general, project sponsor and other stakeholders
answers.explanation: Not an all inclusive list but all stakeholder attitudes in this response are specifically stated along with their attitude toward any proposed solution and team-based collaboration

answers.text: Attitude toward business analysis, project methodology, the sponsor, and project manager
answers.explanation: Attitude toward project methodology is not a listed factor

answers.text: Attitude toward the objective of the initiative, their job, and the organization
answers.explanation: Attitude toward their job and the organization are not listed factors

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements is NOT true about prototyping?

answers.text: When using throw-away or paper prototyping methods, users may feel more comfortable being critical of the mock-up because it is not polished and release-ready.
answers.explanation: True

answers.text: Developers may believe that they must provide a user interface that precisely matches the prototype, even if more elegant technology and interface approaches exist.
answers.explanation: True

answers.text: Prototypes facilitate agreement as a basis for, assessing the completeness and impact of solutions, and defining contractual obligations.
answers.explanation: This is best achieved through scope models

answers.text: Prototypes are used to simulate a new solution so that performance measures can be determined and collected.
answers.explanation: In solution evaluation, prototypes are used to simulate a new solution so that performance measures can be determined and collected

questions.question_text: Given the findings of the team, what would be a reasonable next step?

answers.text: Determine the potential value of the current production and shipping systems.
answers.explanation: This answer describes an input to analyzing performance measures. The team needs to measure solution performance first, and needs to discover business objectives to do that

answers.text: Determine desired business objectives that affect production and shipping.
answers.explanation: The case study lists no business objectives for the initiative. Those would be needed to measure solution performance, a needed first step to determine the effectiveness of the solution relative to its value

answers.text: Determine if the current production and shipping systems are delivering the most potential value.
answers.explanation: This answer is a general one for the entire Solution Evaluation Knowledge Area. Before completing the tasks in evaluating the solution, the business objectives have to be determined. They are an input to measuring solution performance

answers.text: Measure how the current solution is performing relative to the value it provides.
answers.explanation: This is a reasonable answer and would be a logical next step. However, the case study has not provided any business objectives. Those are an input to Measure Solution Performance, which is what this answer is specifying

questions.question_text: Which of the following techniques would be the most helpful to understand the limitations with the current production and shipping processes?

answers.text: Benchmarking and Market Analysis
answers.explanation: This technique is listed as one of the ways to assess solution and enterprise limitations. They will help to understand “if other organizations are experiencing the same solution challenges.”

answers.text: Metrics and Key Performance Indicators
answers.explanation: Metrics and Key Performance Indicators are used to measure solution performance, but they are not cited for assessing solution or enterprise limitations, which the question is asking. If you didn’t memorize that fact, you might remember or figure out that metrics are used for measurements but not listed for assessing them.

answers.text: Focus Groups
answers.explanation: Focus Groups are not listed as a technique to understand system or solution limitations. It would be logical to assume that Focus Groups would help, and perhaps it is a BABOK oversight. But the way to remember this is that Focus Groups are used to gather insights about the current state, which is broader than limitations. , 8.4.6, and 6.1.6

answers.text: Business Capability Analysis
answers.explanation: Business Capability Analysis is used in defining the current state, but not in assessing solution or enterprise limitations. Arguably, Business Capability Analysis could well be used to understand the limitations with the current processes, but the BABOK does not list this technique for understanding limitations. (It is listed as a technique for defining the current state.) , 8.4.6, and 6.1.6

questions.question_text: Which of the following stakeholders will help to understand value assessments for the process improvements and identify ways to improve solution performance and increase value realization?

answers.text: Project Manager
answers.explanation: Project managers are involved with Analyze Performance Measures, but not with “Recommend Actions to Increase Solution Value,” which is what the question is asking

answers.text: Tester
answers.explanation: Testers are involved with Assessing Solution Limitations, but not with “Recommend Actions to Increase Solution Value,” which is what the question is asking

answers.text: Implementation Subject Matter Expert
answers.explanation: This stakeholder is not involved with “Recommend Actions to Increase Solution Value,” which is what the question is asking. Actually they are absent from all Solution Evaluation tasks

answers.text: Domain Subject Matter Expert
answers.explanation: The question is asking about the task “Recommend Actions to Increase Solution Value.” The Domain Subject Matter Expert stakeholder is involved with that task. Besides the Sponsor, the Domain SME participates in each of the tasks in Solution Evaluation

questions.question_text: One of the factors driving the need to reduce costs is that customers are switching to other breakfast products. The effort to reduce costs will help with short-term profits. Besides influencing skills, which of the following competencies will assist you in ensuring that the long-term need for new product lines is addressed?

answers.text: Problem Solving
answers.explanation: The shift in consumer interest from cereal to other breakfast products is a problem. The question is essentially asking to help the organization determine the root cause of why consumers are switching

answers.text: Benchmarking and Market Analysis
answers.explanation: A potential answer, since it would help to study competitors who may offer some of the newer products. But, this answer is a technique, and the question is asking about competencies. Remember to read questions carefully so you don't get tricked by distracters such as this

answers.text: Creative Thinking
answers.explanation: This answer is tempting, but it will be more helpful with generating ideas for the new products rather than convincing executives of the need for alternative products

answers.text: Negotiation and Conflict Resolution
answers.explanation: The case study doesn’t mention or suggest there is conflict. The executives want to increase profits by reducing costs. If that doesn’t work there may be conflict to resolve, but that is an assumption

questions.question_text: The performance of all business analysis activities are directed by which of the following documents?

answers.text: Governance approach
answers.explanation: The governance approach documents which stakeholders will have the authority to make which decisions

answers.text: Business analysis body of knowledge (BABOK)
answers.explanation: The BABOK provides a set of commonly accepted business analysis practices

answers.text: Business analysis approach
answers.explanation: This document identifies the BA approach and activities that will be performed across the initiative including who will perform the activities, the timing and sequencing of the work, the deliverables that will be produced, and the BA techniques that may be utilized

answers.text: Information management approach
answers.explanation: The information management approach documents information storage and access

questions.question_text: Terri has completed her first business analysis package and has to choose the appropriate platform for communicating it to stakeholders. Which of the following is NOT a stated communication platform?

answers.text: Verbal methods
answers.explanation: This is an invented answer

answers.text: Individual collaboration
answers.explanation: This is a stated communication platform along with non-verbal methods and group collaboration

answers.text: Group collaboration
answers.explanation: This is a stated communication platform along with individual collaboration and non-verbal methods

answers.text: Non-verbal methods
answers.explanation: This is a stated communication platform along with individual and group collaboration

questions.question_text: Shirin has been engaged as a Business Analyst in an IT transformation project. While discussing the stakeholder analysis and engagement approach with the Project Manager, they have informed Shirin that since the vendor for this project is using a fixed-price model, the vendor team will be reluctant to do extra work if there is any scope creep during the execution of this project. As a Business Analyst what should Shirin do as a immediate next step?

answers.text: Have a discussion with the Sponsor about the vendor's pricing model and the potential scope creep risk.
answers.explanation: It is a good approach to talk to the Sponsor about any potential risks to the project, but the first step should be to identify any risks to the initiative resulting from stakeholder attitudes or the inability of key stakeholders to participate in the initiative, then talk to the sponsor

answers.text: Understand the vendor's pricing model and note that any scope creep on the project will be a risk and must be managed as such.
answers.explanation: Risk analysis and management is used to identify risks to the initiative resulting from stakeholder attitudes or the inability of key stakeholders to participate in the initiative. In this case the inability of the vendor to do extra work due to scope creep

answers.text: Suggest that the Project Manager review the vendor's pricing model and ask the vendor to change their pricing model to avoid the risk of scope creep.
answers.explanation: This might be a good risk mitigation approach, but the vendor may or may not change their pricing model and at this point Shirin should first capture the risk to the initiative as a risk to be managed

answers.text: Plan to elicit scope and requirements early to avoid any scope creep.
answers.explanation: This might be a good risk mitigation approach, but at this point Shirin should first capture the risk to the initiative as a risk to be managed

questions.question_text: Which subset of stakeholders are likely to participate in the define design options task?

answers.text: Operational support, domain subject matter expert, sponsor
answers.explanation: Sponsor is not one of the stakeholders in this task

answers.text: Operational support, project manager, end user
answers.explanation: End user is not one of the stakeholders in this task

answers.text: Implementation subject matter expert, operational support, supplier
answers.explanation: This is the subset of the stakeholder list for Define Design Options task

answers.text: Implementation subject matter expert, regulator, domain subject matter expert
answers.explanation: Regulator is not one of the stakeholders in this task

questions.question_text: Which of the following criteria are the basis for prioritization of requirements?

answers.text: Issues, benefit, cost, dependencies
answers.explanation: Issues are not a criteria for Basis for Prioritization. The rest are valid criteria

answers.text: Risk, benefit, constraint, dependencies
answers.explanation: Constraint is not a criteria for Basis for Prioritization. The rest are valid criteria

answers.text: Risk, baseline, cost, dependencies
answers.explanation: Baseline is not a criteria for Basis for Prioritization. The rest are valid criteria

answers.text: Risk, benefit, cost, dependencies
answers.explanation: These are the criteria for Basis for Prioritization

questions.question_text: Marie is required to show how elements work in harmony with one another to support the business requirements, and to structure the elements in various ways to align the viewpoints of different stakeholders. What techniques would be useful?

answers.text: Interface analysis, workshops, interviews, organizational modeling, functional decomposition, acceptance criteria.
answers.explanation: Interface analysis and acceptance criteria are not techniques in the Define Requirements Architecture task

answers.text: Workshops, interviews, organizational modeling, functional decomposition, interface analysis, data modeling.
answers.explanation: Interface analysis is not a technique in the Define Requirements Architecture task

answers.text: Workshops, scope modeling, interviews, organizational modeling, functional decomposition, data modeling.
answers.explanation: These are all Define Requirements Architecture techniques

answers.text: Workshops, scope modeling, interviews, organizational modeling, functional decomposition, SWOT analysis, data modeling.
answers.explanation: SWOT analysis is not a technique in the Define Requirements Architecture task

questions.question_text: Tamara, however, thought that user stories would be helpful with which of the following tasks?

answers.text: Analyze Current State.
answers.explanation: User stories will not facilitate an understanding of the current state.

answers.text: Perform Gap Analysis.
answers.explanation: Perform Gap Analysis is not a task.

answers.text: Analyze Performance Measures
answers.explanation: User stories do not help analyze performance measures.

answers.text: Specify and Model Requirements.
answers.explanation: User stories provide a brief statement about what people do or need to do when using the solution

questions.question_text: Specifically, what would user stories help the team thoroughly define and understand?

answers.text: Acceptance Criteria.
answers.explanation: User stories serve to identify specific acceptance criteria to help the team to understand what the solution needs to provide in order to deliver value for the stakeholders

answers.text: Prioritized functional requirements.
answers.explanation: User stories will state functional requirements but from the perspective of a particular role or stakeholder; therefore, an overall prioritization of functional requirements isn't applicable.

answers.text: Risk assessment.
answers.explanation: User stories may assist in the identification or risks but will not help the team "thoroughly define and understand" risks.

answers.text: Non-functional requirements.
answers.explanation: Non-functional requirements typically refer to the entire solution; this response is story-specific.

questions.question_text: Kyle is a BA negotiating a service level agreement (SLA) with a customer associated with a new product currently in development. What type of requirement is Kyle negotiating?

answers.text: Non Functional
answers.explanation: Non-functional requirements define how well the functional requirements must perform

answers.text: Solution
answers.explanation: SLAs are a type of non-functional requirement. Solution requirements include functional requirements

answers.text: Functional
answers.explanation: SLAs are a type of non-functional requirement

answers.text: Transition
answers.explanation: SLAs are a type of non-functional requirement

questions.question_text: Defining an approach for how business analysis data will be stored and accessed represents the purpose of which task?

answers.text: Plan business analysis governance
answers.explanation: The primary purpose of this task is to define how decisions are made

answers.text: Plan business analysis data management process
answers.explanation: There is no such task in the BABOK

answers.text: Plan business analysis approach
answers.explanation: The purpose of this task is to define an appropriate method to conduct business analysis activities

answers.text: Plan business analysis information management
answers.explanation: The primary purpose of this task is to develop an approach for how business analysis information will be stored and accessed

questions.question_text: Which list describes the complete set of tasks in the requirements analysis and design definition knowledge area?

answers.text: Specify and model requirements, verify requirements, validate requirements, define requirements architecture, define design options, analyze potential value and recommend solution
answers.explanation: This is the complete list of tasks. BABOK Figure 7.0.2

answers.text: Specify and model requirements, verify requirements, validate requirements, define design options, analyze potential value and recommend solution
answers.explanation: Define requirements architecture task is missing from this list. BABOK Figure 7.0.2

answers.text: Specify and model requirements, verify requirements, validate requirements, define requirements architecture, assess risks, analyze potential value and recommend solution
answers.explanation: Assess risks is a task in the strategy analysis knowledge area. BABOK Figure 6.0.2

answers.text: Prioritize requirements, specify and model requirements, verify requirements, validate requirements, define design options, analyze potential value and recommend solution
answers.explanation: Prioritize requirements is a task in requirements life cycle management knowledge area. BABOK Figure 5.0.2

questions.question_text: It is essential that stakeholders actively collaborate in the business analysis process so as not to negatively impact the effort. What is the BA's role in ensuring positive collaboration with stakeholders?

answers.text: Set ground rules early in the relationship to provide a safe environment for collaboration.
answers.explanation: Ground rules are one of the elements of successful meeting facilitation, not relationships. The focus in this question is on establishing positive relationships with stakeholders to mitigate the detrimental effects a poor relationship may have on business analysis tasks

answers.text: Establish strong, positive, and trust-based relationships with stakeholders involved in business analysis tasks.
answers.explanation: By developing positive relationships, detrimental effects on business analysis tasks can be modified

answers.text: Escalate concerns about negative stakeholders to the project manager to minimize their effect on the product being produced.
answers.explanation: The question is asking what the BA's role is in ensuring positive collaboration. Escalation is after the fact. The focus in this question is on establishing positive relationships with stakeholders to mitigate the detrimental effects a poor relationship may have on business analysis tasks

answers.text: Listen to stakeholders and incorporate their feedback.
answers.explanation: Listening is part of the answer. Incorporating their feedback may or may not be appropriate. The focus in this question is on establishing positive relationships with stakeholders to mitigate the need for escalation

questions.question_text: Which of the following items should a BA have defined in order to prioritize requirements or assess changes to requirements?

answers.text: Proposed change
answers.explanation: Proposed changes are inputs to Assess Requirements Changes, but not to Prioritize Requirements

answers.text: Requirements
answers.explanation: Requirements (along with designs) are needed inputs to all of the tasks in the Requirements Life Cycle Management knowledge area

answers.text: Potential value
answers.explanation: Potential value is not one of the inputs in the requirements life cycle management knowledge area, and designs is missing from the list. BABOK Figure 5.0.2

answers.text: Change strategy
answers.explanation: Change strategy is not one of the inputs in the requirements life cycle management knowledge area. It is listed as a Guideline/Tool for both Prioritize Requirements and Assess Requirements Changes. BABOK Figure 5.0.2

questions.question_text: In a short, 3-month project to implement a COTS system, the BA determined that a requirements specification document was not necessary and planned instead to develop use cases to document functional requirements. What is this an example of?

answers.text: Making assumptions that may prove risky and cause issues in the project at a later time
answers.explanation: There is no evidence to support this in the scenario.

answers.text: Cutting the wrong corners. Use cases may be used to supplement a more formal specification document
answers.explanation: While use cases may provide supplemental documentation to a requirements document, use cases alone may be the "perfect" way to document functional requirements of the system given the scenario.

answers.text: Incorrectly utilizing use cases
answers.explanation: Use cases do document functional requirements.

answers.text: Determining appropriate requirements analysis and documentation activities
answers.explanation: Use cases articulate the functional behavior of the system

questions.question_text: The CEO questions whether the current manufacturing and related systems can help the company gain new market share. He wants the team to obtain a popular ERP software package called PAS. What is the first thing you should do?

answers.text: Interpret and synthesize the performance measures already reported to analyze the value of the current solution before replacing it.
answers.explanation: There are several solution performance measurements already in the case study. The BA would be wise to use them and start analyzing them, especially since the CEO feels strongly about a solution. It could be argued that additional measurements are needed, but the analysis should point out if additional measurements are needed

answers.text: Estimate the potential value for the PAS option to establish whether it is appropriate to meet the company's needs.
answers.explanation: The answer is essentially the purpose of task 7.6.1, Analyze Potential Value and Recommend Solution. This task is appropriate if there are alternate solution options along with potential value defined. Nothing in the case study supports this action

answers.text: Determine the measures that can be used to evaluate the effectiveness of the software package the CEO wants.
answers.explanation: Rather than “jumping to a solution” which is what the CEO is doing, the BABOK is clear that current solution performance should be measured. Part of that measurement is to assess “how it aligns with enterprise goals and objectives.” The measurements already cited in the case reflect that the current “solution” in place does not support the company’s goals and objectives. Measuring them is not the best answer

answers.text: Develop a change strategy to justify why the software package is the best approach.
answers.explanation: The BABOK mentions that change strategies describe the nature of a change when the current and future states have been defined. It even goes so far as to call a change strategy "justification for why a particular change strategy is the best approach." However, the CEO's desire for a software package represents stated requirements more than a future state

questions.question_text: In responding to the CEO's request, which of the following should be considered?

answers.text: Accuracy, Currency of Data, Sample Size
answers.explanation: Accuracy is an element of analyze solution performance, but the other two are relevant for measuring solution performance. , BABOK 8.2.4

answers.text: Business Objectives, Solution Scope, Implemented Solution
answers.explanation: Business objectives and Implemented Solution are inputs to Measure Solution Performance. Solution Scope is a Guideline for the same task. What is called for is to analyze performance measures. , BABOK 8.2

answers.text: Business Requirements, Metrics and Key Performance Indicators, Organizational Strategy
answers.explanation: These are all inputs or Guidelines for Define Future State, which would come after analyzing the current solution performance measures

answers.text: Risks, Trends, Performance Variances
answers.explanation: What the BA needs to do is analyze performance measures. All of these are elements of that task

questions.question_text: The CIO raises some good points about the current applications. What is potentially lacking with the CIO's considerations?

answers.text: He is considering performance of the applications over their necessary value.
answers.explanation: When analyzing performance measures, an important element is to consider solution performance vs. desired value to the enterprise. Given the negative trend in the data collected, the necessary value may not be present for the current solution

answers.text: He is not taking business needs into account.
answers.explanation: There is nothing to support this answer in the case study. Business need is an input to Analyze Current State, which is not the focus of the case study

answers.text: He does not have quantitative measures to support his concerns.
answers.explanation: This is true, but quantitative measures of the solution performance are listed in the case study. The more salient issue is the analysis of those measures

answers.text: He is focused too much on the business architecture.
answers.explanation: There is nothing to support this answer in the case study. Business architecture is an element of Analyze Current State, which is not the focus of the case study

questions.question_text: To help in understanding the situation and to provide guidance, which of the following techniques would you employ first?

answers.text: Business Case
answers.explanation: This is a tempting answer because a business case usually helps to assess the viability of major initiatives. The reason it is not the correct answer is that competitive analysis would help you to better understand the context and industry trends. It would then help in guiding an eventual business case

answers.text: Industry Knowledge
answers.explanation: This is not a technique, but an Underlying Competency. It would certainly help, but you would likely not have the time to try and increase your knowledge to deal with the situation effectively

answers.text: Business Model Canvas
answers.explanation: True, this technique would help you understand the context for the CEO's requested change and to identify the problems with the biggest impact. The details of the case study already point those out, though, so another answer would be more helpful

answers.text: Competitive Analysis
answers.explanation: Analyzing competitors would help put the industry into perspective. There may be important trends that would affect the assessment of the performance measures mentioned in the case study.  NOTE: In the Techniques chapter Competitive Analysis is called Market Analysis, but is described as analyzing “the factors that influence their decisions to purchase, and the competitors that exist in the market” (10.4.2). In addition, the term Competitive Analysis is listed in the Glossary: “determine the practices of the most significant competitors.”

questions.question_text: The concerns expressed by the CIO can be best summarized by which of the following?

answers.text: He is working to maximize investment.
answers.explanation: This is an invented answer. People might rationalize defending sunk cost by thinking they are trying to maximize investments

answers.text: He is being influenced by sunk cost.
answers.explanation: The mention of investment on existing solutions is a sunk cost. Sunk costs can unduly influence decisions based on time and money spent on existing solutions

answers.text: He is expressing concerns about opportunity cost.
answers.explanation: This might be indirectly true, since pursuing the PAS system prevents working on other solutions that may provide value. There is nothing directly expressed in the case study to support this answer, though

answers.text: He is trying to avoid waste.
answers.explanation: This is a guess based on what the CIO said. He is not talking about removing activities that are wasteful, which if he did would be a valid element of a solution recommendation. There is a better answer

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a key element to consider when conducting elicitation activities?

answers.text: Determine what forms of output the activity will generate.
answers.explanation: This is a stated element within the task of conduct elicitation

answers.text: Consider how the information will be used.
answers.explanation: This is a stated element within the task of conduct elicitation

answers.text: Understand how the output integrates into what is already known.
answers.explanation: This is a stated element within the task of conduct elicitation

answers.text: Select an elicitation technique to use.
answers.explanation: This is not a stated element within the task of conduct elicitation; it is a more appropriate element in the task of preparing for elicitation

questions.question_text: Consider the following Use Case diagram. What is wrong with this diagram?

answers.text: There are too many crossed lines.
answers.explanation: True, there are many crossed lines, but some use case diagrams are complex. This is not a valid issue

answers.text: The Cast Parts use case is connected to too many actors.
answers.explanation: Some use cases will have multiple actors, so this is not a valid issue

answers.text: There are too many actors for the number of use cases.
answers.explanation: There are a modest number of actors, and this is not a valid issue

answers.text: The Marketing actor is not connected to any use case.
answers.explanation: This is a valid issue. All actors on a use case diagram should be connected to at least one use case. Marketing is not connected to any, so it should not appear as an actor

questions.question_text: Which of the following describe common non-functional requirement categories?

answers.text: Reliability, readability, scalability, localization
answers.explanation: Readability is not a non-functional requirements category.

answers.text: Reliability, compatibility, scalability, dependability
answers.explanation: Dependability is not a non-functional requirements category.

answers.text: Reliability, compatibility, scalability, localization
answers.explanation: The 11 other categories listed in the BABOK

answers.text: Reliability, availability, scalability, traceability
answers.explanation: Traceability is not a non-functional requirements category.

questions.question_text: Suzana was attempting to classify the requirements that she had gathered thus far and discovered that she hadn't yet identified the capabilities that the solution must have for the conversion to the future state from the current state. Which classification was she missing?

answers.text: Non Functional
answers.explanation: A classification but one that relates to the quality of the solution, not the conversion from the current to the future state

answers.text: Business
answers.explanation: Business requirements are part of the classification schema, but refer to why a change has been initiated, not how to transition to the future state, which is what Susana is missing

answers.text: Transition
answers.explanation: Transition requirements describe the capabilities that the solution must have and the conditions the solution must meet to facilitate transition from the current state to the future state, but which are not needed once the change is complete

answers.text: Temporary
answers.explanation: The classification that Susana is referring to does describe requirements that are temporary in nature; however, "temporary" is not a classification

questions.question_text: What is the key purpose for maintaining requirements?

answers.text: To ensure organizational assets are updated and managed.
answers.explanation: Requirements are an organizational asset, but there is a better answer

answers.text: To help ensure testing is performed effectively and requirements are traced to test cases.
answers.explanation: Maintaining requirements ensures requirements are accurate and consistent throughout their life cycle and helps support their reuse, it is not to support testing

answers.text: To manage the requirements throughout the requirements life cycle and enable re-use of those requirements in other solutions.
answers.explanation: Maintaining requirements ensures requirements are accurate and consistent throughout their life cycle and helps support their reuse

answers.text: To build a database of requirements that will ensure requirements are not missed in development.
answers.explanation: Not the key purpose but helpful none the less

questions.question_text: Nate is the senior business analyst for the NRO Company. He is working with Rod on completing the requirements prioritization of all the identified requirements. Why would Rod and Nate complete requirements prioritization?

answers.text: To determine which requirements carry the most risk.
answers.explanation: While risk may be a factor to be considered in prioritizing the requirement, it is not, by itself, a justification for completing the prioritization task

answers.text: To determine which requirements should be completed first.
answers.explanation: This is a justification for completing the prioritization task

answers.text: To determine which requirements could be changed.
answers.explanation: This is not a justification for completing the prioritization task

answers.text: To determine who created what requirement based on their position in the organization.
answers.explanation: This is not a justification for completing the prioritization task

questions.question_text: Chris asked Peyton, a BA in the organization, to look a little more closely at BRM performance measurements and recommend a course of action. Peyton began by examining the customer satisfaction scores pre- and post- implementation of the BRM role. She finds there are no questions geared toward new BRM activities. What is her initial recommendation to the CIO?

answers.text: Conduct interviews with the customers that are assigned a BRM to better understand their experience.
answers.explanation: Conducting interviews will allow each customer to be heard and define value independently

answers.text: Conduct a focus group of customers to understand their perceptions of the BRM role.
answers.explanation: This might be a good idea; however, if there are sensitive topics they may not surface. In addition, there is the risk that not everyone would be heard

answers.text: Send a survey to the customers with all questions geared toward the BRM role.
answers.explanation: This wouldn't be as telling given the situation and the size and strategic value of the investment

answers.text: Observe BRMs to ensure their daily work aligns with best practices.
answers.explanation: An internal audit isn't warranted at this time and might create distrust.

questions.question_text: The lead business analyst has assigned you to create requirements models that would help the project team understand roles, responsibilities and reporting structures that exist within the organization and align those structures with the organization's goals. Which requirements model category would you use?

answers.text: People and Roles
answers.explanation: These models represent organizations, groups of people, roles, and their relationships within an enterprise and to a solution

answers.text: Stakeholder List, Map, or Personas
answers.explanation: This is not a category but a technique within the 'People and Roles' category

answers.text: Organizational Modeling
answers.explanation: Close but not the right answer. This is not a category but a technique within the People and Roles category. Beware of such traps!

answers.text: Rationale
answers.explanation: Rationale models represent the 'why' of a change

questions.question_text: Which of the following categories of non-functional requirements are a valid selection?

answers.text: Expendability, security, compliance
answers.explanation: Expendability is not on the list of non-functional requirements

answers.text: Extensibility, usability, performance reliability
answers.explanation: Performance reliability is a made-up phrase

answers.text: Localization, certification, availability
answers.explanation: These are all on the list of non-functional requirements

answers.text: Compliance, security, accuracy
answers.explanation: Accuracy is not on the list of non-functional requirements

questions.question_text: Sofia is a BA who has just helped a business owner define a business need relating to a new service that her company wants to offer. The company is expecting this new service to increase revenues, increase market share, and reduce customer complaints. She, along with key stakeholders, have generated alternative change strategies related to the new service and are completing business cases to support a recommendation. Sofia realizes that she has no idea of the potential business and technical ramifications or magnitude of the effort. She realizes she needs to step back and complete which task/element/technique next?

answers.text: Sofia and her team need to identify risks.
answers.explanation: This is true but is just the first step and so is not comprehensive enough

answers.text: Sofia and her team need to assess risks.
answers.explanation: Risks speak to uncertainty and "potential" impacts

answers.text: Sofia and her team need to analyze constraints, assumptions, and dependencies associated with their recommended solution.
answers.explanation: This is true but all risks need to be analyzed. Analyzing these three things will not address all risks

answers.text: Sofia and her team need to define the potential negative impact to value of the recommended solution.
answers.explanation: This is too specific and is just one component of qualitatively assessing risks

questions.question_text: Laura, a junior business analyst, has finished detailing a set of requirements using analytical techniques. She now wants to elicit, model, and analyze the design, but she is wondering what techniques she would use for this activity. What would you advise her?

answers.text: Since the developers and testers will be using her requirements she should collaborate with them to come up with the best techniques to use.
answers.explanation: Business analysts determine the techniques to use depending on the context, audience and purpose.

answers.text: Requirements and designs use the same techniques to specify and model.
answers.explanation: The same techniques are used to elicit, model, and analyze both. "When the focus of the specifying and modelling activity is on understanding the need, the outputs are referred to as requirements. When the focus of the specifying and modelling activity is on a solution, the outputs are referred to as designs." Task 7.5, "Define Design Options," does not describe analyzing and modeling as does task 7.1, and will have different techniques

answers.text: The business analyst does not create designs but reviews them to ensure they align with the requirements. She should therefore package her artifacts, obtain signoff, and pass them on to a design architect.
answers.explanation: Business analysts have a hand in design

answers.text: The business analyst can only specify and model requirements and not designs.
answers.explanation: Both requirements and designs can be modeled. The defining factor has to do with the focus of the task

questions.question_text: In many cases, stakeholders have different, conflicting needs and expectations. How would a business analyst help expose these differences and ensure their needs are met?

answers.text: Through validation
answers.explanation: The validation process can expose the different, conflicting needs and expectations of stakeholders. Remember that most of the tasks in the BABOK are not sequential, but are iterative and concurrent in nature

answers.text: Through specifying and modeling their requirements
answers.explanation: The purpose of the specify and model requirements task is to analyze, synthesize, and refine elicitation results into requirements and designs. Any differences in understanding the requirements may not occur until validation or review of the requirements

answers.text: Through item tracking
answers.explanation: Item tracking ensures any problems or issues identified are managed and solved, but it will not help expose any problems or conflicting needs

answers.text: Through collaboration
answers.explanation: Collaboration can help resolve issues, but it does not necessarily expose the issue of conflicting needs and expectations between stakeholders

questions.question_text: A conflict may arise among stakeholders as a result of different interpretations of requirements or designs and conflicting values placed on them. Which technique would you use to resolve issues and gain agreement?

answers.text: Workshops
answers.explanation: Workshops may be held with relevant stakeholders to facilitate obtaining approval of requirements and designs

answers.text: Item tracking
answers.explanation: Item tracking will track issues identified during the agreement process but not resolve them

answers.text: Acceptance and evaluation criteria
answers.explanation: This technique is used to define the approval criteria

answers.text: Decision analysis
answers.explanation: Decision analysis examines the possible consequences of alternative outcomes and so is a valuable tool for resolving issues and gaining agreement

questions.question_text: What would the BA likely conduct to determine viability of the BRM role?

answers.text: A decision package.
answers.explanation: This is not a term used in the BABOK.

answers.text: A feasibility study.
answers.explanation: A feasibility study supports the strategic effort and looks at the feasibility and viability of new business opportunities.  and Glossary

answers.text: A market analysis.
answers.explanation: A market analysis involves researching customers to determine what they need or want, factors that influence their decision whether or not to purchase, along with existing competitors for the offering.  This is not what the CIO asked for

answers.text: A business case.
answers.explanation: The CIO wants to determine viability of a particular solution. A business case would include much more than that.

questions.question_text: What might the BA recommend doing before determining the viability of the BRM role?

answers.text: Fully explore, document, and gain consensus on the underlying business need.
answers.explanation: The business need must be understood before exploring potential solutions

answers.text: A benchmarking study to determine how other companies are more cost-effectively addressing the business need.
answers.explanation: This may well end up being a recommendation but we're missing an essential first step.

answers.text: Document the forecasted responsibilities associated with the BRM role in order to better determine viability.
answers.explanation: This may well end up being a recommendation but we're missing an essential first step.

answers.text: A cost/benefit analysis to determine the overall financial value of the BRM role.
answers.explanation: This may well end up being a recommendation but we're missing an essential first step.

questions.question_text: A viewpoint is a set of conventions that define how requirements will be represented, how these representations will be organized, and how they will be related. Which of the following is NOT an example of a viewpoint?

answers.text: Data models and information.
answers.explanation: This is a valid example

answers.text: Views.
answers.explanation: This is the actual requirements and designs for a particular solution from a chosen viewpoint.

answers.text: Audit and security.
answers.explanation: This is a valid example

answers.text: Business process models.
answers.explanation: This is a valid example

questions.question_text: What would be required for a business analyst to align the designed and delivered solutions to the needs to the stakeholders?

answers.text: Understanding enterprise problems and goals, analyzing needs and solutions, devising strategies, driving change, and facilitating stakeholder collaboration.
answers.explanation: All these are activities listed in

answers.text: Understanding enterprise problems and goals, analyzing needs and solutions, driving change, eliminating redundancies, and facilitating stakeholder collaboration.
answers.explanation: Partially correct; eliminating redundancies is not an activity stated in the BABOK

answers.text: Understanding enterprise problems and goals, analyzing needs and solutions, eliminating redundancies, and facilitating stakeholder collaboration.
answers.explanation: Partially correct; eliminating redundancies is not an activity stated in the BABOK

answers.text: Understanding enterprise problems and goals, analyzing needs and solutions, devising strategies, driving change, eliminating redundancies, and facilitating stakeholder collaboration.
answers.explanation: Partially correct; eliminating redundancies is not an activity stated in the BABOK

questions.question_text: A business analyst wants to model all of the data stores, printers, forms, devices; in other words, everywhere data is displayed or shown. Which of the following data flow diagrams would accomplish that goal?

answers.text: Logical data flow diagram.
answers.explanation: These represent the future state regardless of the current physical limitations.

answers.text: Physical data flow diagram.
Context diagram.
answers.explanation: These are too high level and don't reflect where data is stored.

answers.text: A data flow diagram can only show where data is stored.
answers.explanation: This isn't true; a physical data flow diagram would accomplish the goal in the scenario.

questions.question_text: Clair is wrapping up a brainstorming session. Which of the following activities are included in that element?

answers.text: Share ideas, create list, rate ideas, distribute final list
answers.explanation: Sharing ideas occurs during the session itself vs. the wrap-up. This list is missing "discuss and evaluate." BABOK Figure 10.5.1 and 10.5.3

answers.text: Discuss and evaluate, share ideas, create list, and distribute final list
answers.explanation: Sharing ideas occurs during the session itself vs. the wrap-up. This list is missing "rate ideas." BABOK Figure 10.5.1 and 10.5.3

answers.text: Discuss and evaluate, record ideas, rate ideas, and distribute the final list
answers.explanation: Recording ideas occurs during the session itself vs. the wrap-up. This list is missing "create list." BABOK Figure 10.5.1 and 10.5.3

answers.text: Discuss and evaluate, create list, rate ideas, distribute final list
answers.explanation: These are the four activities included in the "wrap - up" element of a brainstorming session. BABOK Figure 10.5.1 and 10.5.3

questions.question_text: Jonathan is the business analyst on an effort and is validating requirements. Which one of the following activities would Jonathan NOT be performing?

answers.text: Identify Solution Validation Criteria.
answers.explanation: Correct. There is no such thing as 'solution validation criteria.'

answers.text: Identify Assumptions.
answers.explanation: This is one of the validation activities, so is not the right answer

answers.text: Define Measurable Evaluation Criteria.
answers.explanation: This is one of the validation activities, so is not the right answer

answers.text: Evaluate Alignment with Solution Scope.
answers.explanation: This is one of the validation activities, so is not the right answer

questions.question_text: On a project a business analyst makes sure that requirements models have met the necessary standard of quality so that they can be used effectively to guide further work. What has the business analyst been doing?

answers.text: Verifying the requirement.
answers.explanation: Verifying makes sure a requirement is 'good enough' to continue working with

answers.text: Documenting a transition requirement.
answers.explanation: Transition requirements describe the capabilities that the solution must have and the conditions the solution must meet to facilitate transition from the current state to the future state, but which are not needed once the change is complete

answers.text: Specifying and modeling the requirement.
answers.explanation: The purpose of specifying and modeling requirements is to analyze, synthesize and refine elicitation results into requirements and designs

answers.text: Validating the requirement.
answers.explanation: Validating is making sure the requirement is warranted and supports the business requirements discovered

questions.question_text: Which solution has the lowest total cost of ownership (TCO) over five years?

answers.text: Custom solution
answers.explanation: The custom solution has a higher TCO of $1,925,000: $1,800,000 + (5*25,000). , 7.6.6, and 10.20.3.2

answers.text: COTS solution
They are equal
answers.explanation: The COTS solution has a lower TCO. , 7.6.6, and 10.20.3.2

answers.text: Not enough information to determine
answers.explanation: An invented answer to distract you. There is enough information. , 7.6.6, and 10.20.3.2

questions.question_text: Given your calculation of Total Cost of Ownership, and assuming equal benefits to the two solutions, which one would you recommend?

answers.text: COTS solution. Even though it has a higher TCO, it will be quicker to implement and benefits will accrue faster.
answers.explanation: The COTS option actually has the lower TCO, so this answer is incorrect. Also, a COTS solution may or may not be quicker to implement.

answers.text: Custom solution. Given how close the two solutions' TCO is, the customized solution offers the greater possibility of fewer adjustments to current processes.
answers.explanation: This answer is reasonable since the TCO of both solutions is roughly equal. A customized solution will in most cases offer the possibility of fewer adjustments to current processes and would be easier to adopt. 7.6.4.4 and 10.20.3.2.

answers.text: Either option. Given the TCO is equal for both options, you should find other criteria on which to base your recommended solution.
answers.explanation: The COTS option actually has a lower TCO, so this answer is incorrect.

answers.text: COTS solution. Given the TCO is equal for both options, the organization is likely to receive benefits quicker with a COTS solution.
answers.explanation: The COTS option actually has the lower TCO, so this answer is incorrect. Also, a COTS solution may or may not be quicker to implement.

questions.question_text: Which of the following best describes the role of acceptance criteria related to a user story?

answers.text: Acceptance criteria are not elicited for user stories but are documented as a way to determine values to be included in test scenarios.
answers.explanation: Invented answer

answers.text: Acceptance criteria are exactly the same as non-functional requirements and are documented at the same time that user stories are refined and approved.
answers.explanation: Invented answer

answers.text: Acceptance criteria relate directly to the entire body of functional requirements vs. just a user story. They are written primarily to speed up the process of writing test scenarios.
