answers.explanation: Acceptance criteria may or may not relate directly to all functional requirements, so this answer cannot be correct. In addition, their main function is not to speed up the writing of test scenarios.

answers.text: Acceptance criteria support the development of user stories to help determine what the solution needs in order to provide value.

questions.question_text: Which of the following techniques would be best suited to identify how to evaluate the quality of the requirements?

answers.text: Reviews
answers.explanation: This does not identify how to evaluate but rather inspects requirements documentation to identify requirements that are not of acceptable quality

answers.text: Verify requirements
answers.explanation: This is a task and not a technique.

answers.text: Metrics and key performance indicators (KPIs)
Acceptance and evaluation criteria
answers.explanation: This is not used to identify how to evaluate but rather ensures that the requirements are stated clearly

questions.question_text: You are the business analyst on an initiative. Which of the following would you consider when determining the optimal approach for conducting business analysis activities?

answers.text: Size of the change
answers.explanation: This is partially true as it is a factor that impacts complexity, but is not a stated element for the plan business analysis approach task which is the task that you are performing

answers.text: Geographic and cultural considerations
answers.explanation: This is partially true as it is a factor that impacts complexity, but is not a stated element for the plan business analysis approach task which is the task that you are performing

answers.text: Complexity and risk
answers.explanation: This is a stated element in the plan business analysis approach task. The other three responses are factors that can impact complexity

answers.text: Number of business areas affected
answers.explanation: This is partially true as it is a factor that impacts complexity, but is not a stated element for the plan business analysis approach task which is the task that you are performing

questions.question_text: Which of the following best describes an element?

answers.text: They are mandatory guidelines regarding how to perform a task or utilize a technique aligned with best practice.
answers.explanation: The BABOK does not contain "mandatory" guidelines

answers.text: They are mandatory guidelines regarding how to utilize a technique aligned with best practice.
answers.explanation: The BABOK does not contain "mandatory" guidelines

answers.text: They are key concepts that are needed to understand how to perform a task or utilize a technique.
answers.explanation: This is the BABOK's definition of an element

answers.text: They are mandatory guidelines regarding how to perform a task aligned with best practice.
answers.explanation: The BABOK does not contain "mandatory" guidelines

questions.question_text: Marie a new hire wants to understand roles, responsibilities, and reporting structures that exist in her organization. Which technique would help her achieve her goal?

answers.text: Matrix model
answers.explanation: This is a type of organizational model vs. a technique.

answers.text: RACI diagram
answers.explanation: A RACI diagram is not a technique (and would not help her understand reporting structures).

answers.text: Organization chart
answers.explanation: This is not a technique but, fundamental diagram used in organizational modeling

answers.text: Organizational modeling

questions.question_text: Steven has conducted a number of elicitation sessions with a group of stakeholders from the Mortgage Loan department for their requirements as part of a new project. How will Steven determine when requirements elicitation from that business group is complete?

answers.text: When Business Analyst have validated that all items defined in the scope of elicitation are completed.
answers.explanation: Defining scope of elicitation allows the Business Analyst to identify when the elicitation activity is complete

answers.text: When all stakeholders from that groups have shared their requirements.
answers.explanation: All stakeholders in business group may not share their requirements

answers.text: When all stakeholders from that group have a shared understanding of the requirements they have provided.
answers.explanation: The stakeholders can have a shared understanding of those requirements they provided, but there may be other requirements to consider that are in scope

answers.text: When no new stakeholders are identified in that business group to share their requirements.
answers.explanation: Defining scope of elicitation allows the Business Analyst to identify when the elicitation activity is complete rather than if all the stakeholders from the same group have been identified and share their requirements

questions.question_text: What will reviewing the organization's structure tell him?

answers.text: It will reflect the formal relationships between people working in the enterprise.
answers.explanation: This is the definition of organizational structure in the BABOK.  & 10.32

answers.text: It will help him calculate the number of communication channels in the organization as input into his communication strategy.
answers.explanation: Close. It will help him understand the formal communication channels but communication is not limited to the formal reporting structure.  & 10.32

answers.text: It will give him insight into the capabilities of the organization.
answers.explanation: The organizational structure does not represent capabilities.  & 10.32

answers.text: It will tell him how many audiences he has to consider when scheduling requirements workshops.
answers.explanation: It is way too early in the analysis process to be planning at a tactical level. Jamey is trying to understand the current state.  & 10.32

questions.question_text: Christopher is analyzing large amounts of data representing varying perspectives in order to identify underlying patterns and relationships that emerge. What technique is Christopher using?

answers.text: Data mining in an unsupervised investigation
answers.explanation: Since patterns and relationships are allowed to emerge from the data, the investigation is "unsupervised."

answers.text: Data canvassing in an unsupervised investigation
answers.explanation: Data canvassing is not a technique.

answers.text: Data mining in a supervised investigation
answers.explanation: Data mining is correct but in a supervised investigation, the analyst begins with a question and looks for an answer that drives decision-making. 10.14.2

answers.text: Data canvassing in a supervised investigation
answers.explanation: Data canvassing is not a technique.

questions.question_text: What is the key difference between a state transition and state diagram?

answers.text: A state diagram is conceptual; a state transition is more detailed and operational.
answers.explanation: State diagrams are not conceptual; both contain details but have varying focuses.

answers.text: A state diagram describes how the entity changes from one state to another; a state transition shows the life cycle of one entity.
answers.explanation: Just the opposite

answers.text: A state transition describes how the entity changes from one state to another; a state diagram shows the life cycle of one entity.
A state transition is conceptual; a state diagram is more detailed and operational.
answers.explanation: State transitions are not conceptual; both contain details but have varying focus.

questions.question_text: Sariah has just completed her required tasks as prescribed by the business analysis plan. Although the project is completed, how can Sariah continue to deliver value to the enterprise?

answers.text: Sariah can create test plans to confirm adoption of the changes by personnel.
answers.explanation: The business analysis plan would've contained information as to how adoption of the solution would be confirmed; the scenario states the tasks within the BA plan are complete

answers.text: Sariah needs to be prepared to conduct analysis in support of enterprise evolution and continuous improvement.
answers.explanation: Business analysis may be performed within the boundaries of a project or throughout enterprise evolution and continuous improvement

answers.text: Sariah needs to conduct a readiness assessment to determine if the organization is ready for additional changes.
answers.explanation: A readiness assessment is conducted with a specific change in mind and would have been done prior to completing all the tasks

answers.text: Sariah does not have any further work to complete as she has done what the project manager has asked of her.
answers.explanation: This is not a true statement as Sariah needs to support the project throughout enterprise evolution and continuous improvement

questions.question_text: Briggs is evaluating the implications of proposed changes to requirements and designs. He needs to use a technique to help him gain an understanding of the impact on the organization or its assets and only has access to a small group of stakeholders. Which technique should Briggs use?

answers.text: Interviews
answers.explanation: The task referenced in this question is the assess requirements changes task. Interviews are used to understand impacts for a single or small group of stakeholders

answers.text: Stakeholder analysis
answers.explanation: Stakeholder analysis is not a stated technique in the BABOK though it is included in stakeholder list, map, or personas technique which is not a stated technique for assessing change impacts

answers.text: Decision analysis
answers.explanation: Decision analysis helps facilitate the change assessment process but does not provide insight to the impact a change request will have on a given or small group of stakeholders

answers.text: Workshops
answers.explanation: Workshops are utilized for larger groups of stakeholders

questions.question_text: Does the predictive or adaptive planning approach place more emphasis on requirements prioritization?

answers.text: Prioritization of requirements is low priority for both approaches.
answers.explanation: Prioritization is a high priority for both planning approaches

answers.text: Both approaches place equal emphasis on requirements prioritization.
answers.explanation: Key word is "more." The answer can't be both

answers.text: Predictive
answers.explanation: The adaptive approach places more emphasis on requirements prioritization as the scope is not fixed

answers.text: Adaptive
answers.explanation: When utilizing the adaptive approach, a prioritized requirements list may be the only requirements document. The scope is not fixed in a project using an adaptive approach

questions.question_text: According to the BABOK, what is business analysis?

answers.text: The practice of enabling change in an enterprise by defining needs and recommending solutions that deliver value to stakeholders.
answers.explanation: Business analysis enables an enterprise to articulate needs and the rationale for change, and to design an describe solutions that can deliver value

answers.text: A set of stakeholder needs that are elicited, analyzed, structured, and specified for use in the design or implementation of a solution.
answers.explanation: This answer is designed to sound good, but is misleading because of the focus on design and implementation

answers.text: A collection of pre-project or early project activities and approaches for capturing the necessary view of the business to provide context to requirements and functional design work for a given initiative and/or for long-term planning.
answers.explanation: This is too definitive and refers mainly to strategy analysis. Business analysis is not always performed within the context of a project

answers.text: The process of eliciting, documenting, and managing requirements throughout the life cycle of a project.
answers.explanation: Business analysis is not a process, it is the practice of enabling change in an enterprise by defining needs and recommending solutions that deliver value to stakeholders

questions.question_text: What type of task is the team performing during this final meeting with the stakeholders?

answers.text: Formal walkthrough
answers.explanation:
This answer is tempting since the case study mentions the team will walk the stakeholders through the model. However, a “Formal walkthrough” is a type of “Review” technique, but it is not a task. (It is worth knowing the types of reviews, by the way.)

The broader task described by the case is that of verifying requirements. The description of the team wanting to “make sure the model meets internal standards and will function as the business intends” paraphrases the description of Verify Requirements. , 10.37.3.2

answers.text: Validating requirements
answers.explanation: Validate Requirements focuses on ensuring that requirements and designs align to business requirements and provide value. The purpose of this final review with the stakeholders was to make sure the data requirements were ready for development

answers.text: Defining requirements architecture
answers.explanation: Define Requirements Architecture is concerned with how “requirements collectively support one another to fully achieve the objectives.” It focuses on the structure and organization of all the requirements rather than reviewing the requirements for quality, accuracy, and usability

answers.text: Verifying requirements
answers.explanation: The team’s reason to meet with stakeholders is to ensure that the data requirements are ready for development. The case description would indicate the requirements are going through verification. According to the BABOK: “The purpose of Verify Requirements is to ensure that requirements and designs specifications and models meet quality standards and are usable for the purpose they serve.”

questions.question_text: As you review orders and shipments with the subject matter experts, which of the following statements best describes a data business rule in the model?

answers.text: An order can be shipped on any number of shipments, and the items can be split up.
answers.explanation: This statement is supported by the relationships between orders and shipments. , 10.15.3.3

answers.text: A location can be designated as the default shipping location.
answers.explanation: Not currently present. But, if the business needs this feature, a location can be designated as the default shipping location by adding an attribute to the Location entity. , 10.15.3.2

answers.text: An order item can be shipped to multiple locations.
answers.explanation: The relationship between an Order Item and Location shows the item can be assigned to only a single location. The double line symbol (||) designates this  and Figure 10.15.1

answers.text: An order can be shipped on any number of shipments, but each item must be shipped on one shipment.
answers.explanation: An Order Item may have multiple Order Fulfillments, making this answer incorrect. The “crow’s foot” symbol designates this cardinality rule.  and Figure 10.15.1

questions.question_text: Suppose a stakeholder asks what the limitations are imposed by this model. How would you respond?

answers.text: A contact can work only at a single location.
answers.explanation: Not true since a Contact Location permits a contact to work at multiple locations. The “crow’s foot” symbol designates this cardinality rule

answers.text: A customer must have at least one order on file to be valid.
answers.explanation: Not true since a Customer may place zero to many Orders. The “0” on the crow’s foot from Customer to Order denotes this

answers.text: There is no way to identify a specific individual who a shipment is intended for.
answers.explanation: True, there is no entity or attribute to designate this fact

answers.text: A location must be able to accept shipments to be valid.
answers.explanation: Not true since a Location may accept zero to many Shipments. The “0” on the crow’s foot from Location to Shipment denotes this

questions.question_text: Given the model as it currently exists, what information might you want to elicit from your stakeholders during the final review?

answers.text: Confirm that a contact can only work at one location.
answers.explanation: The model currently shows a Contact can be at multiple Locations, so this would not make sense to ask. It’s possible that you might want to potentially challenge every relationship, but presumably by this point the relationships have been verified. Note: The Contact Location entity is the one that allows multiple Locations per Contact.

answers.text: Ask why a customer must have one order to be a valid customer.
answers.explanation: The model actually shows that a Customer can be added even before they place an Order, so this would not be an appropriate question to ask. The “0” on the crow’s foot from Customer to Order denotes this.

answers.text: Ask why a location must be able to accept orders to be considered valid.
answers.explanation: The model actually shows that a Location does not have to accept Orders to be valid, since a Location may not accept shipments. For example, a Location might be an administrative office, so this would not be an appropriate question to ask. The “0” on the crow’s foot from Location to Order Item denotes this.

answers.text: Confirm that an order can potentially be split up.
answers.explanation: The model supports this and since it may appear as a mistake, it would be sensible to confirm that the business wants this feature. , 10.15

questions.question_text: To help understand the most important areas of the model to focus the development efforts on first, which approach would the BA use to prioritize the necessary business analysis work?

answers.text: Rank the entities from most to least important.
answers.explanation: Ranking is a valid prioritization approach, but there is nothing in the case study to suggest which criteria would be used to do the ranking. , 10.33.3

answers.text: Reach consensus by negotiating which entities to work on first.
answers.explanation: Negotiation is a valid prioritization approach, but more information would be needed to use this approach, such as which entities would provide the most business value. It would also take additional stakeholder involvement and time, something it does not sound like they want to do. , 10.33.3

answers.text: Use a budgeting/time-boxing approach.
answers.explanation: This seems the most sensible approach given the implied tight timeframe mentioned. Budgeting/time boxing helps prioritize based on a fixed resource of time or money. , 10.33.3

answers.text: Ask the product owner to group the entities in high, medium, and low priority.
answers.explanation: Grouping by high, medium and low priority is a valid approach to prioritization but the implied tight timeframe would likely negate this method. Also, having the product owner do the grouping is not something the BABOK would prescribe. , 10.33.3

questions.question_text: Which tools provide a means of documenting business analysis information and are easy to share with stakeholders?

answers.text: Business analysis tools
answers.explanation: Modeling and diagraming tools may not be easy to share with stakeholders.

answers.text: Configuration management tools
answers.explanation: This is a generic name for requirements management tools. They may not be easy to share with stakeholders.

answers.text: Requirements management tools
answers.explanation: These are meant for documenting and managing requirements, but may not be easy to share with stakeholders.

answers.text: Office productivity tools
answers.explanation: Office productivity tools and technology can be used to document and track information and artifacts

questions.question_text: Your organization has chosen to enforce two new rules as a matter of policy in order to enhance productivity. What type of rule has your organization enforced?

answers.text: Definitional rules.
answers.explanation: In contrast to behavioral rules, which are about the behavior of people, definitional rules represent operational knowledge of the organization

answers.text: Employee rules.
answers.explanation: This is a made up answer. Refer to

answers.text: Behavioral rules.
answers.explanation: Behavioral rules (also known as an Operative rules; see Glossary page 441) are rules the organization chooses to enforce as a matter of policy, often to reduce risk or enhance productivity

answers.text: Guidelines.
answers.explanation: This is behavioral rule for which there is no active enforcement. Not the best answer

questions.question_text: Sallie the BA was in the process of conducting training and noticed that members of the class were having difficulty retaining the information. She is currently using PowerPoint slides supported by written material. What might you suggest she try?

answers.text: Assessments; they would pay better attention if they knew they were going to be tested.
answers.explanation: The BABOK does not list assessments as a retention tool.

answers.text: More frequent review of the material.
answers.explanation: This would employ the same auditory (implied by the fact that she is conducting training) and visual techniques (PowerPoint slides) she is currently using.

answers.text: Include more graphics.
answers.explanation: There is no evidence that graphics aren't being used today; graphics still employ auditory (implied by the fact that she is conducting training) and visual (PowerPoint slides) learning styles which don't appear to be helping students retain material.

answers.text: Add a kinesthetic component.
answers.explanation: Kinesthetic (learning by doing) is the only learning technique Sallie doesn't appear to be using at this time. Combining techniques helps people learn faster and retain information longer

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a component of the risk analysis and management technique?

answers.text: Ease of detectibility of a risk
answers.explanation: This is a component of FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) but is not a recognized component of the risk analysis and management technique in the BABOK

answers.text: Risk response plan
answers.explanation: Formulating a risk response is part of risk management

answers.text: Likelihood of occurrence
answers.explanation: Likelihood of occurrence is part of risk analysis; also called probability

answers.text: Impact of a risk
answers.explanation: Assessing the impact of a risk is part of risk analysis

questions.question_text: A core competency to using tools and technology effectively is?

answers.text: The ability to select the most appropriate tool that will meet the needs of the audience.
answers.explanation: The ability to select the most appropriate tool to meet the needs of the audience is a base in all business analysis tools and technology core competencies. ; 9.6.2.3; 9.6.3.3

answers.text: the ability to reduce the time or cost to implement a requirements change.
answers.explanation: This core competency is listed under Solution Knowledge

answers.text: Track any tool or technology issues and to report them to the project manager.
answers.explanation: This is a made up competency.

answers.text: The ability to understand and to use some of the major features of the tool.
answers.explanation: A business analyst should be able to use most of the major features of key business analysis tools and technology.

questions.question_text: What are the outputs that you are looking to achieve by prioritizing requirements?

answers.text: Requirements (Prioritized), Design (Approved)
answers.explanation: Both requirements and designs should be prioritized. BABOK Figure 5.3.1 and Section 5.3.8

answers.text: Requirements (Prioritized), Design (Prioritized)
answers.explanation: As depicted in BABOK Figure 5.3.1 and Section 5.3.8

answers.text: Requirements (Approved), Design (Prioritized)
answers.explanation: Both requirements and designs should be prioritized. BABOK Figure 5.3.1 and Section 5.3.8

answers.text: Requirements (Designed), Design (Prioritized)
answers.explanation: Both requirements and designs should be prioritized. BABOK Figure 5.3.1 and Section 5.3.8

questions.question_text: What sort of assessment are you able to perform without the current state description?

answers.text: You can assess value from both the solution and enterprise perspectives.
answers.explanation: The current state description is needed to assess value from the enterprise perspective

answers.text: Solution limitations.
answers.explanation: Solution limitations can be assessed without the current state description

answers.text: You are not able to assess value from either the solution or enterprise perspectives without the current state.
answers.explanation: You are able to assess value from the solution perspective without the current state

answers.text: Enterprise limitations.
answers.explanation: The current state description is needed as an input into assessing any enterprise limitations that may be prohibiting the end-users from experiencing the full value of the solution

questions.question_text: To complete a thorough assessment, you want to review any enterprise limitations that may be preventing the end-user from obtaining full value from the solution. To this end, you need to gather any information related to the current state. What types of factors will you need to consider?

answers.text: A description of the current limitations of the enterprise including how the solution performance is impacting the enterprise.
answers.explanation: This is an an output of the assess enterprise limitations task. Before you assess the enterprise limitations, you need a current state description

answers.text: Any and all solution limitation factors including defects and constraints.
answers.explanation: You have already assessed the solution limitations, you are now focusing on enterprise limitations.

answers.text: Any current environmental, cultural, and internal factors that may be influencing the solution limitations.
answers.explanation: These factors are included in the current state description listed as an input into assessing enterprise limitations

answers.text: The results from the solution performance analysis including collected measurements and any recommendations to solve performance gaps and leverage opportunities to improve value.
answers.explanation: You already have the solution performance analysis results, so your focus is getting an understanding the current state

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT an improvement opportunity when defining design options?

answers.text: Eliminate redundancy
answers.explanation: This is an improvement opportunity.

answers.text: Improve access to stakeholders
answers.explanation: Not mentioned as an improvement opportunity

answers.text: Improve access to information
answers.explanation: This is an improvement opportunity.

answers.text: Increase consistency of behavior
answers.explanation: This is an improvement opportunity.

questions.question_text: Which of the following LEAST describes why an activity diagram may be utilized?

answers.text: An activity diagram is effective at showing how to handle a large number of scenarios and parallel branches.
answers.explanation: This accurately describes one of the benefits of an activity diagram.

answers.text: An activity diagram can elaborate on a single use case.
answers.explanation: This accurately describes one of the benefits of an activity diagram.

answers.text: An activity diagram facilitates the identification of potential improvements by highlighting "pain points" in the process structure.
answers.explanation: This accurately describes one of the benefits of an activity diagram.

answers.text: An activity diagram utilizes business process model and notation (BPMN) so it can distinguish the activities of different participants in a process with either pools or swim lanes.
answers.explanation: An activity diagram utilizes unified modeling language (UML)

questions.question_text: Sarah documented what circumstances must be in place prior to the steps outlined in a use case she was creating. What term addresses what Sarah was documenting?

answers.text: Alternative flow
answers.explanation: An alternative flow describes a scenario or other path that could be followed to arrive at the post-condition, but which is different from the primary or "happy" path. One can think of an alternative flow as a slight "detour!"

answers.text: Precondition
Boundaries
answers.explanation: Pre and post conditions define the boundaries of the use case, so while this is partially correct, it is does not answer the question.

answers.text: Post condition
answers.explanation: This term describes what is true when the use case is completed.
